qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XSS via insufficient URL sanitization allows javascript: protocol execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes infinite recursion in the block tokenizer triggered by \x09\x0b\n input. This finding is about URL sanitization in cleanUrl() failing to block javascript: protocol schemes. The vulnerable code, root cause, and attack path are entirely different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via automatic CLI config file loading
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes infinite recursion in the block tokenizer triggered by \x09\x0b\n input during parsing. This finding is about the CLI automatically loading config files from ~/.marked.js. The vulnerable code (bin/main.js config loading vs lib/marked.esm.js tokenizer) and root cause are completely unrelated.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes an infinite recursion vulnerability in marked's block tokenizer triggered by a specific 3-byte input (\x09\x0b\n) in lib/marked.esm.js. Neither qualifying finding identifies this root cause: xss-url-sanitization addresses URL protocol filtering in helpers.ts, and cli-code-execution addresses CLI config auto-loading in bin/main.js. No qualifying finding describes the tokenizer's handling of vertical tab characters or the blockTokens→inlineTokens recursion loop.
evidence: Advisory root cause: space() tokenizer consumes \x09, then \x0b\n falls through to text tokenizer, triggering infinite recursion between blockTokens() and inlineTokens()
xss-url-sanitization: cleanUrl() in helpers.ts, completely different code path and root cause
cli-code-execution: runConfig() in bin/main.js, completely different code path and root cause
